recent_coach_change: Yes — Miroslav Koubek replaced Ivan Hasek in December 2025. At 74, Koubek is the oldest manager at the 2026 World Cup; he is a former goalkeeper who won the Czech league with Viktoria Plzen.
qualification_path: Qualified via the European playoffs, beating Republic of Ireland (2-2, won 4-3 on penalties on 26 March) and then Denmark (2-2, won 3-1 on penalties on 31 March), both in Prague and both on penalty shootouts.
key_players: Patrik Schick (Bayer Leverkusen) — leading striker; scored in the warm-up win over Guatemala
Ladislav Krejci (Wolves) — captain and defensive leader
Tomas Soucek (West Ham) — central midfield engine
Adam Hlozek (Hoffenheim) — forward returning from a long calf/foot injury layoff
Pavel Sulc — attacking midfielder, one to watch

injuries_doubts.issue: Missed most of the club season with calf and foot injuries; recovered to be included and tested in warm-ups
Ankle injury sustained in warm-up against Kosovo; coaching staff expected to manage him carefully before the tournament

squad_notes: Koubek named the final 26 on 31 May 2026, with Wolves defender Ladislav Krejci as captain. Spine of the side: Matej Kovar in goal, Tomas Holes in defence, Tomas Soucek in midfield and Patrik Schick up front, plus Adam Hlozek, Pavel Sulc and Lukas Provod. Hlozek's return from a long injury layoff was a notable inclusion.
warmup_assessment: Solid finish to warm-ups: Czechia beat Guatemala 3-1 on 4 June 2026 in Harrison, New Jersey (Schick, Tomas Chory and Denis Visinsky scoring), after an earlier match against Kosovo in which Jan Kuchta picked up an ankle knock.
shape_verdict: Czechia return to the World Cup after a 16-year absence carrying late momentum from a 3-1 win over Guatemala, with Schick fit and firing and Hlozek back from injury, though they only scraped through via two playoff penalty shootouts under new 74-year-old coach Miroslav Koubek.

news.headline: Czechia beat Guatemala 3-1 in World Cup warm-up; Schick, Chory and Visinsky score
Miroslav Koubek names final 26-man Czechia squad with Ladislav Krejci as captain
Adam Hlozek returns from long-term calf and foot injury to make Czechia's preliminary World Cup squad
